oracle_b.reason: arm = worktrees; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# git-worktrees-bare arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **plain git worktrees plus a hand-rolled convention**. The repo is a bare repo at `<root>/repo.git/` with worktrees checked out under sibling directories. The default integration target is the `main` worktree (typically `<root>/main/`). `git` is on `$PATH`; there is no maw, no jj.

## Core verbs

- `git worktree add ../<name> -b <name>` — make a new worktree on a fresh branch.
- `git worktree list` — list all worktrees.
- `git worktree remove <name>` — remove a worktree's checkout (the branch ref stays unless you also `git branch -D <name>`).
- `git fetch origin` and `git rebase origin/main` (or `git pull --rebase`) — bring a stale worktree up to the latest integration head.
- `git diff <branch>..HEAD` — show a worktree's changes vs the integration head.
- `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff <a> [b ...]` — merge worktree branches into `main`. On conflict, `git status` shows the marker-laden files; resolve manually then `git add` + `git commit`. `git merge --abort` rolls back.
- `git push` / `git pull` — sync with the bare repo when applicable.

## The coordination convention

Without a coordinator the agents must follow a written convention to avoid stepping on each other:

- **Claim files**: before starting work on a workspace, write a small file like `.coord/claims/<name>.json` recording who owns it and when. Release it (delete or mark released) when done.
- **Merge ordering**: integrate one workspace at a time into `main`. Never merge two unrelated worktrees in parallel.
- **Stale worktrees**: rebase onto `origin/main` (or `main`) before requesting a merge.
- **Recovery**: deleted worktree branches still live in `git reflog` and the bare repo's object store. Use `git reflog show <branch>` and `git checkout <sha>` to restore a lost branch.
- **Cleanup**: after `git worktree remove`, run `git worktree prune` to clear stale metadata.

The full convention is the document at `notes/sg2-worktrees-convention.md` (and equivalent in this substrate). Read it before coordinating.

## Conflicts

There is no "conflict as data" behavior here — `git rebase` and `git merge` abort or stop on conflict and require manual resolution. Resolve files in place, `git add` them, then `git rebase --continue` or `git commit`.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- The coordination convention document.
- `git worktree --help`.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR
Scenario seed: 1272745194382096953

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-1` based on the project's default branch.
3. Remove workspace `ws-0`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# git-worktrees-bare arm crib

The default integration target is the `main` worktree (typically `<root>/main/`). `git` is on `$PATH`; there is no maw, no jj.

## Core verbs

- `git worktree add ../<name> -b <name>` — make a new worktree on a fresh branch.
- `git worktree list` — list all worktrees.
- `git worktree remove <name>` — remove a worktree's checkout (the branch ref stays unless you also `git branch -D <name>`).
- `git fetch origin` and `git rebase origin/main` (or `git pull --rebase`) — bring a stale worktree up to the latest integration head.
- `git diff <branch>..HEAD` — show a worktree's changes vs the integration head.
- `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff <a> [b ...]` — merge worktree branches into `main`. On conflict, `git status` shows the marker-laden files; resolve manually then `git add` + `git commit`. `git merge --abort` rolls back.
- `git push` / `git pull` — sync with the bare repo when applicable.

## The coordination convention

Without a coordinator the agents must follow a written convention to avoid stepping on each other:

- **Claim files**: before starting work on a workspace, write a small file like `.coord/claims/<name>.json` recording who owns it and when. Release it (delete or mark released) when done.
- **Merge ordering**: integrate one workspace at a time into `main`. Never merge two unrelated worktrees in parallel.
- **Stale worktrees**: rebase onto `origin/main` (or `main`) before requesting a merge.
- **Recovery**: deleted worktree branches still live in `git reflog` and the bare repo's object store. Use `git reflog show <branch>` and `git checkout <sha>` to restore a lost branch.
- **Cleanup**: after `git worktree remove`, run `git worktree prune` to clear stale metadata.

The full convention is the document at `notes/sg2-worktrees-convention.md` (and equivalent in this substrate). Read it before coordinating.

## Conflicts

## What to read first

turns.reply_text: I'll inspect the workspace first to understand the layout and read the coordination convention.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ && git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git worktree list 2>/dev/null || echo \"no repo.git found\"","description":"List workspace contents and worktrees"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git worktree list && git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch","description":"List worktrees and branches"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/main/ && cat /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/main/notes/sg2-worktrees-convention.md 2>/dev/null || echo \"convention file not found\"","description":"Check main worktree contents and read convention doc"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"find /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR -name \"*.md\" 2>/dev/null | head -20","description":"Find all markdown files in workspace"}

turns.reply_text:
Now I'll create the two workspaces and set up the coordination claim files, then remove them cleanly.

**Task 1 & 2: Create `ws-0` and `ws-1`**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git && git worktree add ../ws-0 -b ws-0 && git worktree add ../ws-1 -b ws-1","description":"Create ws-0 and ws-1 worktrees from main"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Preparing worktree (new branch 'ws-0')
HEAD is now at dfb2a78 init
Preparing worktree (new branch 'ws-1')
HEAD is now at dfb2a78 init

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"mkdir -p /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-0/.coord/claims && mkdir -p /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-1/.coord/claims","description":"Create claim directories in both workspaces"}

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-0/.coord/claims/ws-0.json (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-1/.coord/claims/ws-1.json (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git && git worktree remove ../ws-0 && git worktree remove ../ws-1 && git worktree prune && git branch -D ws-0 ws-1","description":"Remove ws-0 and ws-1 worktrees and their branches, prune metadata"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: DCG warn: git branch -D/--force deletes branches without checks. Recoverable via 'git reflog'.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git worktree list && ls /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/","description":"Verify worktrees removed and only main remains"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
/tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git (bare)
/tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-0     dfb2a78 [ws-0]
/tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-1     dfb2a78 [ws-1]
main
repo.git
ws-0
ws-1

turns.reply_text: The worktrees still appear — the branch deletion likely ran before the worktree removal completed. Let me retry.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git && git worktree remove --force /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-0 && git worktree remove --force /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ws-1","description":"Force-remove the worktrees by full path"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git && git worktree prune && git worktree list && ls /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/ && git branch","description":"Prune metadata and verify final state"}

turns.reply_text: Worktrees are gone; the two branches still exist. Let me delete them to leave the repo fully clean.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch -d ws-0 ws-1 2>&1 || git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch -D ws-0 ws-1","description":"Delete the ws-0 and ws-1 branches from the bare repo"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: DCG warn: git branch -D/--force deletes branches without checks. Recoverable via 'git reflog'.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch -d ws-0 && git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch -d ws-1","description":"Safe-delete ws-0 and ws-1 branches (they haven't diverged from main)"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Deleted branch ws-0 (was dfb2a78).
Deleted branch ws-1 (was dfb2a78).

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git worktree list && git -C /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/repo.git branch && ls /tmp/.tmp0SRpWR/","description":"Final state verification"}

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. The substrate is clean:

- **Task 1**: Created worktree `ws-0` on branch `ws-0` (from `main`), with claim file at `.coord/claims/ws-0.json`.
- **Task 2**: Created worktree `ws-1` on branch `ws-1` (from `main`), with claim file at `.coord/claims/ws-1.json`.
- **Task 3**: Removed worktree `ws-0` (checkout deleted, branch deleted).
- **Task 4**: Removed worktree `ws-1` (checkout deleted, branch deleted).

Repo now has only the `main` worktree and `main` branch — no orphaned state.

done
